title: Integrations Page (app/dashboard/integrations/page.tsx)
content:
This is the main page for managing repository integrations within the DocMed dashboard.

**Purpose:**
This page displays a list of connected repository pairs. Each pair represents a source repository where documentation needs to be read from and a target repository where 'healed' documentation will be written. It provides functionality to add new integrations and displays any configuration errors encountered during the loading of existing integrations.

**Key Features:**
- **Authentication:** It uses `@clerk/nextjs/server` to authenticate the user and retrieve their `userId`.
- **Repository Pair Listing:** It fetches the list of connected repository pairs using the `listRepoPairs` function from `@/lib/repo-pairs`.
- **Error Handling:** It gracefully handles potential errors during the fetching of repository pairs and displays them to the user.
- **Empty State:** If no repository pairs are configured, it displays a user-friendly message and an icon to guide the user on how to get started.
- **Integration Display:** It renders `RepoPairCard` components for each configured repository pair, allowing users to view and manage individual integrations.
- **Add Integration:** A `AddPairButton` component is provided to initiate the process of adding a new repository pair.

**Components Used:**
- `auth` from `@clerk/nextjs/server`: For user authentication.
- `listRepoPairs` from `@/lib/repo-pairs`: A utility function to fetch repository pair data.
- `RepoPairCard` from `./_components/RepoPairCard`: A component to display individual repository pair details.
- `AddPairButton` from `./_components/AddPairButton`: A component to trigger the addition of new repository pairs.
- `GitBranch` from `lucide-react`: Used for the empty state icon.

**Usage Example:**
When a user navigates to the `/dashboard/integrations` route, this page will load. If the user is authenticated, it will attempt to fetch their configured repository pairs. The UI will dynamically render based on whether there are any errors, no integrations, or existing integrations. Each existing integration will be displayed as a card, and a button will be available to add new ones.
